'''Michael Monaghan''' (born 13 May 1980) is the current assistant coach at the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]]<ref name="Monaghan: 'Philosophy filtered throughout the grades'">{{cite web|url=https://www.seaeagles.com.au/news/2018/11/09/monaghan-philosophy-filtered-through-the-grades/|title=Monaghan: 'Philosophy filtered throughout the grades|publisher=Sea Eagles|date=2018-11-13}}</ref> and an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played for the [[Canberra Raiders]] and [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in the [[National Rugby League]], and for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/><ref name=warr>[http://www.warringtonwolves.org/9-michael-monaghan--7 Profile at Warrington Wolves site] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100706035554/http://www.warringtonwolves.org/9-michael-monaghan--7 |date=6 July 2010 }}</ref>

==Background==
He is the brother of former [[Australian national rugby league team|Australia international]] [[Joel Monaghan]].<ref name="canberra" />

==Playing career==

===Canberra Raiders===
Monaghan played with the [[Canberra Raiders]] from 2001 to 2003. He made his first grade debut in round 16 of the [[2001 NRL season]] against [[Cronulla-Sutherland Sharks|Cronulla]] at [[GIO Stadium|Bruce Stadium]]. Monaghan's last appearance for the club was Canberra's 17-16 elimination finals loss against the [[New Zealand Warriors]] during the 2003 NRL finals series.<ref name=canberra/>

===Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles===
Monaghan joined [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles]] in 2004 as {{rlp|hb}}, but changed to {{rlp|hk}} at the start of the 2006 season due to the purchase of {{rlp|hb}} [[Matt Orford]] from Melbourne Storm.<ref name=warr/>

He played in the [[National Rugby League season 2007#Grand final|2007 NRL grand final]] defeat against the [[Melbourne Storm]]. This title was later stripped due to the [[Melbourne Storm salary cap breach]].<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.news.com.au/dailytelegraph/story/0,22049,24415751-5001023,00.html| work=The Daily Telegraph|location=Sydney| title=Factfile on 2007 grand final| date=2008-09-29}}</ref>

===Warrington Wolves===
[[file: Michael Monaghan.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Monaghan playing for Warrington in 2008]]
The 27-year-old joined Manly teammate [[Chris Hicks (rugby league)|Chris Hicks]] in moving to [[Warrington Wolves]] for 2008's [[Super League XIII]].<ref name=" Warrington sign Manly wing Hicks">{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_league/super_league/warrington/6225294.stm|title= Warrington sign Manly wing Hicks|publisher=BBC|date=2007-06-21|access-date=2007-06-21}}</ref>

In March 2009 rumours emerged linking him with his former club Canberra and playing alongside brother [[Joel Monaghan]] again.<ref name=canberra>{{cite web|url=http://www.canberratimes.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/canberra-pursuing-homesick-halfback/1456985.aspx|title=Canberra pursuing homesick halfback|author=David Jean|work=The Canberra Times|date=2009-03-12|access-date=2011-09-15}}{{dead link|date=January 2018 |bot=InternetArchiveBot |fix-attempted=yes }}</ref>

Monaghan played for [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] in the [[2009 Challenge Cup|2009]] and [[Challenge Cup 2010#Final|2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victories over [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] and [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] respectively, winning the [[Lance Todd Trophy]] for his Man-of-Match performance in 2009.<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/8938067.stm|title=Leeds 6-30 Warrington|publisher=BBC|date=28 August 2010|access-date=22 November 2019|first=Ged|last=Scott}}</ref>

Monaghan played in the [[2010 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref name="Leeds 6-30 Warrington" /><ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |location=London |work=The Guardian |title=Chris Hicks hat-trick leads Warrington to Challenge Cup triumph over Leeds |first=Andy |last=Wilson |date=28 August 2010 |access-date=22 November 2019<!--DASHBot--> |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20100831105934/http://www.guardian.co.uk/sport/2010/aug/28/leeds-rhinos-warrington-wolves-challenge-cup-final |archive-date=31 August 2010 |url-status=live }}</ref>

Monaghan played in the [[2012 Challenge Cup Final]] victory over Leeds at [[Wembley Stadium]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/aug/25/leeds-warrington-challenge-cup |title=Warrington's battered Brett Hodgson recovers to see off Leeds in final|date=25 August 2012|work=The Guardian|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19362692|title=Leeds Rhinos 18–35 Warrington Wolves|date=25 August 2012|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref> He also played in the [[2012 Super League Grand Final]] against Leeds at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|title=Grand Final: Warrington 18-26 Leeds|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/19805055|access-date=20 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=6 October 2012}}</ref><ref name="Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2012/oct/06/warrington-leeds-grand-final|title=Leeds' Kevin Sinfield stars in Grand Final triumph against Warrington|work=The Guardian|date=6 October 2012|access-date=20 November 2019}}</ref>

Monaghan played in the [[2013 Super League Grand Final]] defeat against [[Wigan Warriors|Wigan]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref>{{cite news|last=Newsum|first=Matt|title=Super League Grand Final: Wigan Warriors beat Warrington|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/24384930|access-date=13 November 2019|publisher=BBC Sport|date=5 October 2013}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url = https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2013/oct/05/warrington-wigan-super-league-grand-final|title= Wigan see off Warrington in X-rated Grand Final to complete double|date=5 October 2013|work=The Guardian |access-date=13 November 2019}}</ref>

In May 2014, Monaghan announced he would be retiring at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|title=Michael Monaghan: Warrington hooker announces retirement|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/27651973|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=24 December 2014|date=31 May 2014}}</ref>

==Coaching career==

===Catalans Dragons===
He then joined the [[Catalans Dragons]] as assistant coach for the 2015 season<ref name="Monaghan takes reins at Catalan (sic) Dragons">{{cite web|url=https://www.sbs.com.au/news/monaghan-takes-reins-at-catalan-dragons|title=Monaghan takes reins at Catalan (sic) Dragons|publisher=sbs|date=2017-05-24}}</ref> and took temporary charge in 2017 with fellow assistant [[Jerome Guisset]] following the French club's decision to part company with head coach [[Laurent Frayssinous]].

===Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles===
In 2018 Monaghan re-joined former club [[Manly-Warringah Sea Eagles|Manly]] as Pathways / Specialist coach.<ref name="Monaghan: 'Philosophy filtered throughout the grades'" />

==Honours==

===Warrington Wolves===
*[[Challenge Cup]](3): [[2009 Challenge Cup Final|2009]], [[2010 Challenge Cup Final|2010]], [[2012 Challenge Cup Final|2012]]

===Individual awards===
* 2006 Ken Stephen award.
* 2009 Lance Todd Trophy
